chunks.text: IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH, KARACHI Cr. Misc. Application No.1100 of 2025 Present: Mr. Justice Muhammad Iqbal Kalhoro Justice Ms. Sana Akram Minhas Applicant:- Zafar Iqbal through Mr. Muhammad Farooq, Advocate. Respondent:- The State through Ms. Rubina Qadir, Deputy Prosecutor General. Date of hearing:- 26.03.2026 =============== MUHAMMAD IQBAL KALHORO J: Through this application, applicant who claims to be owner of white Toyota Corolla GLI bearing Registration No.BEV-812 is seeking restoration of said vehicle involved in Crime No.1359/2024 of PS Shah Latif Town, Karachi to his possession on the ground that he is registered owner of the vehicle and pending trial the same may be released to him on Supardari. His counsel has argued that before registration of present FIR, the applicant had got a report registered in daily diary of PS Moro about theft of his vehicle, which is much prior to the narcotics case registered on 06.10.2024. 2. Learned DPG has however opposed the application 3. Be that as it may, in terms of last order, the report by Excise & Taxation Department has been received which shows that applicant is the registered owner of the said car. It is an admitted position that initially applicant’s involvement in the narcotics case was suspected but later he was let-off by the police. More so, there is a report of the police of PS Moro showing that before the FIR in narcotics case, the said car was stolen from Moro. 4. Under the circumstances, we allow this application and restore possession of said vehicle to applicant against surety in the sum of Rs.10,00,000/- with the condition that he should submit the original documents in the trial Court with further direction that the said vehicle shall be produced in the trial as and when required and applicant shall not create any third party interest in respect of vehicle in question.

More so, there is a report of the police of PS Moro showing that before the FIR in narcotics case, the said car was stolen from Moro. 4. Under the circumstances, we allow this application and restore possession of said vehicle to applicant against surety in the sum of Rs.10,00,000/- with the condition that he should submit the original documents in the trial Court with further direction that the said vehicle shall be produced in the trial as and when required and applicant shall not create any third party interest in respect of vehicle in question. 5. This order is tentative in nature, the trial Court would be competent to pass the final order in respect of the property including this vehicle in the judgment as required by law. The Criminal Misc. Application is accordingly disposed of in above terms. JUDGE HANIF JUDGE
